letters.label: Letter One
variants:
My name is Pip. I am eight.

I am on a train I dont know. The seats smell like Grandmas coat.

A rat gave me this paper. A real rat. With clothes.

I am looking for Mum.

A little bit scared. Only a little bit.

Pip

I fell asleep on a train with Mum. I woke up and Mum was gone.

The train is different now. My bag is different. The orange book is not in it.

A rat gave me this paper. He had a vest.

I am going to find Mum. There are so many carriages.

I am not crying.

Pip

letters.label: Letter Two
variants:
I asked a man where the conductor is.

He had a yellow coat. He laughed. He said sweetheart there is no conductor. He said the train does not stop.

I dont believe him. Trains stop.

The man in the yellow coat sleeps a lot now. I think he is sad.

I am not going to be like that.

Pip

I found a notebook under a seat.

Someone counted the carriages. They got to 384 and stopped. They said there is no point looking for the front.

I dont know what they meant. Trains have a front.

I am keeping the notebook. It is like having someone to talk to.

They sounded tired. I am not tired yet.

Pip

letters.label: Letter Three
variants:
Some of the people on the train are pretend.

They look real. But they dont see you. I waved at a lady and she walked right past me.

Some people ARE there. The man in the yellow coat. The rat. A lady who gave me an apple.

I am keeping a list of the real people. There are four. I am probably one too.

Pip
